skill.name: libtv-skill
skill.description: Use LibTV and liblib.tv to create, edit, upload, query, and download AI-generated images and videos from natural language requests.
skill.summary: Create and edit AI images and videos through the LibTV OpenAPI workflow.
skill.author: libtv-labs

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported 119 patterns, including network calls, environment access, filesystem writes, shell examples, and weak-crypto heuristics. Most high-severity crypto and command findings are false positives from documentation or identifier text, but the skill intentionally sends credentials, prompts, and media to external LibTV endpoints. Publish with a warning that users should trust the configured API base URL and confirm local files before upload.

medium_findings.title: External API Calls Handle Prompts and Credentials
medium_findings.description: The helper reads LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY and sends bearer-authenticated requests to an API base URL that can be changed by environment variables. This is required for LibTV, but a hostile environment value could redirect prompts and credentials.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly builds Authorization headers from LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY and uses an environment-controlled base URL. The behavior is legitimate for this API client but sensitive.

medium_findings.title: Local Media Files Can Be Uploaded to LibTV
medium_findings.description: The upload script reads a user-supplied local file and sends the file bytes plus access key to the upload endpoint. MIME filtering limits intended use to images and videos, but users must avoid uploading sensitive local media.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script opens the local path and includes the bytes in a multipart request. The intent matches the skill, so this is a controlled but real data-transfer risk.

medium_findings.title: Downloaded Results Are Written to Local Paths
medium_findings.description: The result downloader retrieves URLs and writes files into a default or user-selected output directory. The direct URL option can fetch arbitrary supplied URLs, so downloaded content should be treated as untrusted.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes and URL download path are explicit. Risk depends on user-provided URLs and output paths, so confidence is high for behavior and moderate for exploitability.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The repeated shell-command findings in SKILL.md are fenced usage examples that show how to invoke Python scripts. They are not executable code inside the skill package, although an agent may run them as part of the documented workflow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text appears inside Markdown code fences and documentation examples. No Ruby backticks or shell execution APIs are present in the inspected Python files.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Heuristics Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic functions. The flagged lines are descriptions, UUID identifiers, or output fields used for project and session IDs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted search found no weak cryptographic API use. uuid.uuid4 is used only to create a multipart boundary, not for authentication or encryption.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Heuristics Are Benign Path Handling
low_findings.description: The system-reconnaissance findings map to script path setup, argument parsing, and project UUID handling. No evidence found of host inventory, process listing, network scanning, or sensitive system enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected lines import os, add the script directory to sys.path, and handle returned project IDs. These are normal Python packaging patterns.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment-Controlled API Base URL
dangerous_patterns.description: OPENAPI_IM_BASE or IM_BASE_URL can override the default LibTV endpoint, which can redirect authenticated requests if the environment is untrusted.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The override is explicitly documented and implemented. It is useful for configuration but creates credential-routing risk in hostile environments.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bearer Token Sent on Network Requests
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill sends LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY in Authorization headers for API and upload requests.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Authorization header construction is direct and documented. This is expected but must be disclosed because it handles credentials.

dangerous_patterns.title: Arbitrary Direct URL Download Option
dangerous_patterns.description: download_results.py accepts explicit URLs and writes their contents into the chosen output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL list and file-writing behavior are explicit. The primary workflow extracts LibTV result URLs, but the manual option broadens input trust.

content.user_title: Create AI Images and Videos with LibTV
content.value_statement: Creative teams need a repeatable way to send media requests to LibTV without manual API work. This skill creates sessions, uploads references, tracks progress, and downloads generated results.
actual_capabilities: Create a new LibTV session and send a natural language image or video request.
Send follow-up prompts to an existing session by session ID.
Upload local image or video references before an edit or generation request.
Query session progress and return generated image or video messages.
Download generated media files to a local output directory.
Switch the project linked to the configured LibTV access key.
limitations: Requires a valid LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY environment variable.
Sends prompts and uploaded media to external LibTV services.
Long video, short drama, or MV tasks can take several minutes.
Does not create prompts locally; it passes user requests to the backend agent.

use_cases.title: Generate Campaign Videos
use_cases.description: Create product videos from a short natural language brief and download the finished media for review.

use_cases.title: Edit Existing Media
use_cases.description: Upload a reference image or video and request object replacement, style changes, or shot adjustments.

use_cases.title: Build Storyboard Assets
use_cases.description: Generate storyboard frames or short visual sequences for planning a larger creative project.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Image
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an image of a modern desk setup with soft daylight and clean product styling.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Short Video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a five-second video of a coffee cup on a table while morning light moves across the scene.

prompt_templates.title: Edit Uploaded Media
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload this reference video, then replace the paper boats with white paper hearts and keep the camera motion.

prompt_templates.title: Produce a Multi-Step Storyboard
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this reference image to create a 16:9 nine-panel storyboard showing the next scene in the same visual style.

output_examples.input: Generate a product showcase video for a smart lamp on a clean desk.
output_examples.output: The skill creates a LibTV session, sends the request, polls for completion, and returns media links plus downloaded local files.

output_examples.input: Change the object in my uploaded video from a paper boat to a paper heart.
output_examples.output: The skill uploads the reference video, sends the edit instruction with the uploaded URL, then retrieves the generated result.

output_examples.input: Download all generated images from this finished session.
output_examples.output: The skill extracts result links from session messages and saves the files into the selected output folder.

best_practices: Use trusted API endpoint environment variables before running the skill.
Confirm that local reference files are intended for upload.
Poll long-running video jobs at reasonable intervals and stop after repeated failures.
anti_patterns: Do not upload private images or videos without explicit user approval.
Do not rewrite the user prompt with extra creative details unless requested.
Do not download arbitrary URLs from untrusted messages into sensitive folders.

faq.question: What account or key is required?
faq.answer: You need a LibTV access key set in the LIBTV_ACCESS_KEY environment variable.

faq.question: Does this skill send data to an external service?
faq.answer: Yes. It sends prompts, uploaded media, and API requests to the configured LibTV endpoint.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing video?
faq.answer: Yes. Upload the video first, then send the edit request with the uploaded media URL.

faq.question: Can it download finished results?
faq.answer: Yes. It can extract generated media URLs from session messages and save the files locally.

faq.question: Does it improve prompts before sending them?
faq.answer: No. The instructions tell the agent to pass the user request through to the backend workflow.

faq.question: Why is the security rating medium?
faq.answer: The skill handles credentials, uploads local media, downloads files, and uses external network APIs.
